description_long: This stereo sound system features a robust, boxy main body in matte black, with a stepped, tiered top section and a small protruding knob or antenna at the center. On one side, a prominent red circular component is attached via a pair of cables, suggesting a detachable or remote control unit, possibly a wireless speaker or control pad. The front face includes a rectangular section that may house a speaker grille or control interface, and a small metallic or reflective accent is visible. The left side has a brownish-gold panel, likely for connectivity or power input, and a thin wire extends from this area. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, focusing on function over form. The materials appear to be primarily plastic with some metal and rubber components, suitable for urban environments such as garages, parks, or street corners.
description: A boxy black stereo sound system with a red circular remote or speaker attached by cables, featuring metallic and brown accent panels.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main black body, a small metallic accent, and the red circular component partially visible on the left.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the red circular component fully, attached by cables, and a brownish panel on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the black stepped body and the red circular component with cables extending outward.
description_view_3: Right side view shows the brownish panel and the red circular component with cables looping to the left.
